report_metadata.report_title: Unpacking BellaCiao: A Closer Look at Iran's Latest Malware

ip_addresses.evidence_snippet: Threat actors also attempted to download two IIS backdoors from http://188.165.174[.]199:18080.

file_hashes.evidence_snippet: first one was a build of IIS-Raid, a native IIS module (MD5:5a487c41efa2f3055d641591d601977c) downloaded from

tools.name: BellaCiao
tools.evidence_snippet: Custom dropper malware tailored to individual targets; hardcoded victim info (company name, subdomains, IP); named after Italian resistance song; disables Defender; drops IIS backdoors + Plink/PowerShell reverse proxy

tools.name: IIS-Raid
tools.evidence_snippet: Native IIS module backdoor; processes requests for headers X-Beserver-Verify (password Pss.XxYyTtGg123!) and X-Forward-Verify (command); MD5 5a487c41efa2f3055d641591d601977c

tools.name: Plink
tools.evidence_snippet: Used for reverse proxy connections to C2; executed via wmic.exe; multiple MD5 hashes

tools.name: PowerShell Web Server
tools.evidence_snippet: Hardcoded scripts implement HTTP server for command execution, file upload/download, beep, report time

cves.evidence_snippet: the initial attempts to exploit CVE-2022-47966 in Zoho ManageEngine were identified

summary: Bitdefender Labs analysis (April 26, 2023) of BellaCiao malware by Charming Kitten (APT35/Mint Sandstorm/IRGC): custom dropper for targeted Exchange servers (US/EU/Middle East/India); disables Defender; masquerades as Exchange services; drops IIS-Raid backdoor (headers X-Beserver-Verify=Pss.XxYyTtGg123!, X-Forward-Verify=cmd) + .NET cred exfil module (X-Verify-Request=01odmkfnPAnjf); Plink reverse proxy + PowerShell web server. C2 via unique DNS IP parsing (subdomain 2randUpper+3randLower; compares resolved vs hardcoded IP; last octet instructs webshell deploy/remove/disappear). Primary targets: Exchange servers via ProxyShell/ProxyNotShell/OWA-SSRF. IRGC post-2021 aggression: quick PoC weaponization (Log4Shell took weeks, recent CVEs same-day).

timeline.event: Charming Kitten first identified targeting political dissidents/activists/journalists
timeline.evidence_snippet: Charming Kitten also known as APT35/Mint Sandstorm/PHOSPHORUS is an Iranian state-sponsored APT group associated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps IRGC... has been on the radar of the infosec community since 2014

timeline.event: Ebrahim Raisi speech praises IRGC cyber capabilities; marks IRGC aggression increase
timeline.evidence_snippet: In a speech on 17 March 2021, Ebrahim Raisi then chief justice of Iran declared The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has excelled in every field it has entered

timeline.event: Raisi elected president; IRGC/APT groups adopt aggressive approach post-transition
timeline.evidence_snippet: In August 2021, Raisi replaced more moderate candidate Hassan Rouhani as the president of Iran. Starting only one month after his inauguration, cyberattacks attributed to IRGC threat actors started increasing
